edges.description: Failure of cohesion, axis formation, and synapsis leaves homologs unpaired and recombination unresolved, triggering prophase I arrest of the affected germ cells.
intermediate_mechanisms: defective meiotic recombination and DNA repair
edges.source: Meiotic cohesin instability and synaptonemal complex disruption
edges.target: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell apoptosis

edges.description: In 46,XX individuals, oocyte loss during meiotic progression exhausts the ovarian follicle pool and produces primary ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell apoptosis
edges.target: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: In 46,XY individuals, the same germ-cell arrest produces spermatogenic failure with absence of mature sperm.
edges.source: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell apoptosis

edges.description: The female branch presents clinically as primary / premature ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

edges.description: The male branch presents clinically as non-obstructive azoospermia.

edges.target: Meiotic cohesin instability and synaptonemal complex disruption

nodes.description: STAG3 is the meiosis-specific stromal-antigen subunit of the cohesin ring and is the only meiosis-specific cohesin subunit present in all four meiosis-specific cohesin complexes. Biallelic STAG3 loss destabilizes the other meiotic cohesin subunits and disrupts their localization to the chromosome axes, impairing meiotic sister-chromatid cohesion and assembly of the synaptonemal complex that synapses homologous chromosomes.
nodes.id: Meiotic cohesin instability and synaptonemal complex disruption

evidence.reference_title: Mutant cohesin in premature ovarian failure.
evidence.snippet: STAG3 encodes a meiosis-specific subunit of the cohesin ring, which ensures correct sister chromatid cohesion.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the stromal antigen 3 (STAG3) gene cause male infertility due to meiotic arrest.
evidence.snippet: In both women and men, STAG3 encodes for a meiosis-specific protein that is crucial for the functionality of meiotic cohesin complexes.

evidence.reference_title: Meiosis-specific cohesin component, Stag3 is essential for maintaining centromere chromatid cohesion, and required for DNA repair and synapsis between homologous chromosomes.
evidence.snippet: The instability of meiosis-specific cohesins observed in Stag3 mutants results in aberrant DNA repair processes, and disruption of synapsis between homologous chromosomes.

evidence.reference_title: Meiosis-specific cohesin component, Stag3 is essential for maintaining centromere chromatid cohesion, and required for DNA repair and synapsis between homologous chromosomes.
evidence.snippet: STAG3 is the only meiosis-specific subunit that is represented within all four meiosis-specific cohesin complexes.

nodes.description: Affected 46,XY individuals present with non-obstructive azoospermia due to complete meiotic arrest of spermatogenesis.
Loss of oocytes during meiotic prophase depletes the ovarian reserve, presenting clinically as primary / premature ovarian insufficiency and primary amenorrhea in 46,XX individuals.
nodes.id: Ovarian follicle depletion and primary ovarian insufficiency

evidence.reference_title: Mutant cohesin in premature ovarian failure.
evidence.snippet: Female mice devoid of Stag3 are sterile, and their fetal oocytes are arrested at early prophase I, leading to oocyte depletion at 1 week of age.

evidence.reference_title: Whole-exome sequencing identifies a homozygous donor splice-site mutation in STAG3 that causes primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Two homozygous germline truncation mutations in STAG3 gene had been reported to causes POI in consanguineous families.

nodes.description: Affected 46,XX individuals present with primary / premature ovarian insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Whole-exome sequencing identifies a homozygous donor splice-site mutation in STAG3 that causes primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Two homozygous germline truncation mutations in STAG3 gene had been reported to causes POI in consanguineous families.

nodes.description: Germ cells that fail to synapse and complete meiotic recombination arrest during meiotic prophase I and are eliminated by apoptosis rather than maturing into functional gametes, depleting the germ-cell pool in both sexes.
nodes.id: Prophase I meiotic arrest and germ cell apoptosis

evidence.reference_title: Meiosis-specific cohesin component, Stag3 is essential for maintaining centromere chromatid cohesion, and required for DNA repair and synapsis between homologous chromosomes.
evidence.snippet: These defects result in early prophase I arrest and apoptosis in both male and female germ cells.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the stromal antigen 3 (STAG3) gene cause male infertility due to meiotic arrest.
evidence.snippet: germ cells did not develop further than zygotene and showed drastic chromosome aberrations.

evidence.reference_title: Mutant cohesin in premature ovarian failure.
evidence.snippet: STAG3 encodes a meiosis-specific subunit of the cohesin ring, which ensures correct sister chromatid cohesion.

evidence.reference_title: STAG3 homozygous missense variant causes primary ovarian insufficiency and male non-obstructive azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: We report the first pathogenic homozygous missense variant in STAG3 and the first STAG3 variant associated with both male and female infertility.

nodes.description: In 46,XY individuals, STAG3 loss arrests spermatocytes in meiotic prophase I, producing complete meiotic arrest and non-obstructive azoospermia.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the stromal antigen 3 (STAG3) gene cause male infertility due to meiotic arrest.
evidence.snippet: Sequence variants affecting protein function of STAG3 cause male infertility due to meiotic arrest.

evidence.reference_title: STAG3 homozygous missense variant causes primary ovarian insufficiency and male non-obstructive azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: Here, we study a case of familial infertility including a proband with POI and her brother with NOA.
